edges.description: Loss of the heme a cofactor yields a catalytically inactive enzyme.
edges.source: COX10 Loss and Defective Heme A Biosynthesis
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy deficit in the CNS produces Leigh-type encephalopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Bioenergetic failure in muscle contributes to weakness in COX10-related mitochondrial disease.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Impaired oxidative phosphorylation shifts pyruvate metabolism toward lactate accumulation, producing lactic acidosis.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

nodes.description: Biallelic COX10 variants disrupt heme O synthase, the first step of heme A biosynthesis, depriving COX of its heme a cofactor and preventing assembly of a functional holoenzyme.
nodes.id: COX10 Loss and Defective Heme A Biosynthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in COX10 result in a defect in mitochondrial heme A biosynthesis and account for multiple, early-onset clinical phenotypes.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in COX10 result in a defect in mitochondrial heme A biosynthesis

evidence.reference_title: Complex mitochondrial disease caused by the mutation of COX10 in a toddler: a case-report study.
evidence.snippet: This mutation led to a deficiency in COX10, which is a component of mitochondrial complex IV.

nodes.description: Leigh syndrome / progressive encephalopathy due to COX10-related COX deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Biallelic COX10 Mutations and PMP22 Deletion in a Family With Leigh Syndrome and Hereditary Neuropathy With Liability to Pressure Palsy.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic COX10 Mutations and PMP22 Deletion in a Family With Leigh Syndrome and Hereditary Neuropathy With Liability to Pressure Palsy.

nodes.description: Loss of functional COX blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: Mammalian cytochrome c oxidase (COX) catalyses the transfer of reducing equivalents from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane.

nodes.description: Elevated lactate from impaired oxidative metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Cytochrome c oxidase deficiency: patients and animal models.
evidence.snippet: Human diseases associated with COX deficiency including encephalomyopathies, Leigh syndrome, hypertrophic cardiomyopathies, and fatal lactic acidosis are caused by mutations in COX subunits or assembly factors.

nodes.description: Muscle weakness, a presenting feature in a COX10-deficient toddler.

evidence.reference_title: Complex mitochondrial disease caused by the mutation of COX10 in a toddler: a case-report study.
evidence.snippet: A 3-year-old girl was admitted due to muscle weakness
